title: User Molecules Storage with RLS
sql:
-- 1. Create the molecules storage table
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.user_molecules (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    data JSONB NOT NULL,
    settings JSONB NOT NULL,
    is_favorite BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    last_viewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE(user_id, name)
);

-- 2. Enable Security (RLS)
ALTER TABLE public.user_molecules ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Users can manage their own saved molecules." 
ON public.user_molecules FOR ALL USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 3. Add persistence columns to User Settings
ALTER TABLE public.user_settings 
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS last_molecule TEXT,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS last_molecule_settings JSONB;

-- 4. Enable Realtime (optional but recommended)
ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE public.user_molecules;
description: Creates a per-user molecules table to persist molecule data and preferences (including favorites and timestamps), enforces uniqueness by user/name, adds RLS so users can only access their own records, extends user_settings with last-viewed molecule info, and publishes the table for Supabase Realtime updates.

title: Add Message Read Timestamp
sql: ALTER TABLE public.support_messages ADD COLUMN read_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NULL;
description: Alters the support_messages table to add a nullable read_at column (timestamp with time zone) for recording when each support message was read.

title: Self-Serve Deletion for Support Data
Add Attachment Fields to Support Messages
sql:
ALTER TABLE support_messages
ADD COLUMN attachment_url TEXT,
ADD COLUMN attachment_name TEXT,
ADD COLUMN attachment_type TEXT;
description: Updates the support_messages table to store attachment details by adding columns for the attachment URL, file name, and MIME/content type.

title: Update Support Admin Message Read Status
sql:
-- Allow users to update messages (to mark admin messages as read)
CREATE POLICY "Users can update messages setting is_read"
    ON public.support_messages FOR UPDATE
        USING (
                conversation_id IN (
                            SELECT id FROM public.support_conversations WHERE user_id = auth.uid()
                                    ) AND sender_type = 'admin'
                                        )
                                            WITH CHECK (
                                                    conversation_id IN (
                                                                SELECT id FROM public.support_conversations WHERE user_id = auth.uid()
                                                                        ) AND sender_type = 'admin' AND is_read = true
                                                                            );
description: Defines a row-level security policy that lets authenticated users update only support messages sent by admins within their own conversations, allowing them to mark those admin messages as read.

title: Support Conversations and Messages Schema
sql:
-- 1. Create support_conversations table
CREATE TABLE public.support_conversations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    user_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('chat', 'mail')),
    subject TEXT, -- mail mode ke liye use hoga, chat ke liye null
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open' CHECK (status IN ('open', 'closed', 'pending')),
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

-- 2. Create support_messages table
CREATE TABLE public.support_messages (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    conversation_id UUID REFERENCES public.support_conversations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    sender_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, 
    sender_type VARCHAR(20) CHECK (sender_type IN ('user', 'admin')),
    message TEXT NOT NULL,
    is_read BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

-- Turn on Row Level Security
ALTER TABLE public.support_conversations ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.support_messages ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- 3. RLS Policies for support_conversations
-- Users can see their own
CREATE POLICY "Users can view their own support conversations"
    ON public.support_conversations FOR SELECT
    USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Users can insert their own
CREATE POLICY "Users can create their own support conversations"
    ON public.support_conversations FOR INSERT
    WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- For admin panel, assuming admins have a specific role or access list. 
-- For now, if Admin panel bypassed via service role key, RLS is automatically bypassed.
-- But if using user token, create a policy allowing admins (e.g., using an admin_users table) to select/update.

-- 4. RLS Policies for support_messages
-- Users can see messages in their own conversations
CREATE POLICY "Users can view messages in their conversations"
    ON public.support_messages FOR SELECT
    USING (
        conversation_id IN (
            SELECT id FROM public.support_conversations WHERE user_id = auth.uid()
        )
    );

-- Users can insert messages in their conversations
CREATE POLICY "Users can insert messages in their conversations"
    ON public.support_messages FOR INSERT
    WITH CHECK (
        conversation_id IN (
            SELECT id FROM public.support_conversations WHERE user_id = auth.uid()
        ) AND sender_type = 'user' AND sender_id = auth.uid()
    );

-- Enable Realtime
-- Admin aur User done bina delay k messages and tickets details haasil karein
ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE support_conversations;
ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE support_messages;
description: Creates tables to manage user support tickets and their message history, including conversation metadata (type, subject, status), message details (sender, read flag, timestamps), and Row Level Security policies so users can only view and create content within their own conversations. Also registers both tables for Supabase Realtime updates.

title: Add Broadcast Classification Columns
sql:
ALTER TABLE public.broadcasts 
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS type VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'popup',
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS banner_type VARCHAR(50),
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT false;
description: Updates the broadcasts table by adding new fields to categorize broadcast type and banner type, along with an is_active flag to control whether a broadcast is currently enabled.

title: Add Default Broadcast Type
description: Adds a new `type` column to the `public.broadcasts` table (defaulting to `'popup'`) and updates existing rows with `NULL` values to ensure they all have a valid broadcast type.

title: Add Dismissible and Display Type Fields
description: Alters the broadcasts table to include two new attributes—whether a broadcast can be dismissed and what type of display it should use—with default values set to true and 'popup' respectively.

title: Idempotent Conversations Schema Migration
Add user_id foreign key to conversations
sql:
ALTER TABLE public.conversations 
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS user_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE;
description: Adds a user_id UUID column to the public.conversations table (if it doesn’t already exist) and links it to auth.users(id), automatically removing related conversation rows when the user is deleted.

title: Add Broadcast Expiration Timestamp
sql: ALTER TABLE public.broadcasts ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS expires_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE;
description: Adds an optional expires_at column to the public.broadcasts table to store when a broadcast should expire, including time zone information.

title: News API Key & Processing Auditing Extensions
description: Adds cooldown/error tracking columns to the existing news_api_keys table, creates audit and article-processing log tables for recording failures, fallbacks, and skip reasons, enables RLS with admin policies, and defines SECURITY DEFINER RPC functions to mark key usage/failures and to insert audit/skip log records.

title: News API Key Success/Failure Cooldown Management
description: Defines two PL/pgSQL functions to manage news API key usage: resets failure/cooldown state and marks a key active on success, and increments failure count on failure, computing an exact provider-specific daily cooldown reset time when max failures are reached (setting the key to exhausted with cooldown_until).

title: News API Key Tracking & Activity Logging
description: Creates a public.news_api_keys table to store API credentials and usage stats, enables RLS with full-access policy for authenticated users, adds an event trigger that automatically attaches row-level logging triggers to newly created tables, and defines SECURITY DEFINER functions to atomically increment call counts and failure counts while transitioning keys to an exhausted status.

title: Add Account Name to API Keys
sql: ALTER TABLE public.news_api_keys ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS account_name TEXT;
description: Alters the public.news_api_keys table to add an optional account_name column (only if it doesn’t already exist) so each stored API key can be associated with an account name.

title: Get Public Tables With Sequence Flag
sql:
-- Pehle purane function ko drop karein
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.get_all_tables();

-- Ab naya function create karein
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_all_tables()
RETURNS TABLE(table_name text, has_sequence boolean) AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN QUERY 
    SELECT 
        t.tablename::text,
        EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 
            FROM information_schema.columns c 
            WHERE c.table_schema = 'public' 
              AND c.table_name = t.tablename 
              AND (c.column_default LIKE 'nextval(%' OR c.is_identity = 'YES')
        ) AS has_sequence
    FROM pg_tables t
    WHERE t.schemaname = 'public';
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;
description: Creates/replaces a security-definer function that lists all tables in the public schema and returns each table name along with a boolean indicating whether it has an auto-increment sequence (via nextval() default or identity columns).

title: Enable Realtime Updates for News System Config
sql:
-- Enable realtime for the news_system_config table
alter publication supabase_realtime add table news_system_config;
description: Adds the news_system_config table to the supabase_realtime publication so changes to its rows are broadcast for real-time updates.

title: API Key Activity Logging for News Providers
Add Activity Logs to Realtime Publication
sql: ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE activity_logs;
description: Adds the activity_logs table to the supabase_realtime publication so changes to this table are included in realtime updates/subscriptions.

title: News System Configuration Store
description: Creates a configuration table for storing key/value JSON settings used by the news system, seeds default model configuration records, and sets up idempotent row-level security policies to control read/update/insert/delete access for authenticated users.

title: News System Configuration Store
description: Creates a public.news_system_config table to store configurable settings (key/value JSONB) for the news system, seeds default formatting and summary model entries, and enables Row-Level Security with policies allowing authenticated users to read and write configuration while preserving controlled access.

title: News API Keys & Activity Auditing Setup
description: Creates and extends the public.news_api_keys table to manage multiple providers’ API keys with usage/failure tracking, cooldowns, and enhanced status fields. Also adds an api_key_audit_logs table for recording failed/fallback attempts, installs an event trigger that attaches per-table row-level activity triggers automatically, enables RLS with permissive policies for authenticated users, and defines SECURITY DEFINER RPCs to mark keys as used/failed and to log audit entries.

title: Activity Audit Logging with Auto-Triggers
description: Creates an activity_logs table plus PL/pgSQL functions and triggers to automatically record INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE changes (old/new row data) across all public tables, prune the log to the latest 1000 entries, and attach the same logging trigger to future table creations. Also includes a helper RPC function for manually logging frontend events.

title: Broadcast table
description: Sets up 'broadcasts' and 'broadcast_iterations' tables to manage mass communications. It tracks email drafts, AI-generated content versions, and delivery status flags.

title: Add Conversation Metadata Columns
sql:
ALTER TABLE public.conversations 
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is_voice_conversation BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS audio_url TEXT,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS summarization_failed BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS summaries JSONB DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS planner_context JSONB DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is_pinned BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE;
description: Updates the conversations table to store voice-related settings and data (voice flag, audio URL), summarization status and outputs (failure flag, summaries JSONB), planner context (JSONB), and UI/behavior state (pinned flag), adding columns only if they don’t already exist.

title: Dairy Tables Schema Updates
description: Updates the dairy-related tables by adding missing columns for payment item linkage (item_id with FK to dairy_items), payment method, and item icon. Also relaxes dairy_entries.item_id to be nullable to allow syncing payments when item_id is initially blank.

title: User Settings Storage & RLS Setup
description: Creates and resets the public.user_settings table to store per-user configuration (voice modes, API key, translator usage), enables Row Level Security so users can only manage their own rows, and updates the handle_new_user trigger function to automatically create a corresponding user_settings record on signup.

title: Profiles, Settings, and Avatar Storage Setup
Database Analytics with Last Used Timestamp
description: Defines and grants a Postgres function that returns per-table statistics from pg_stat_user_tables (live row count and total inserts/updates/deletes) and adds a computed last_used column by finding the maximum value of likely timestamp columns (e.g., updated_at/created_at/last_sign_in_at) in each public table.

title: Drop Table Utility
description: Defines a SECURITY DEFINER PL/pgSQL function that dynamically drops a specified table (if it exists) and automatically cascades dependent objects.

title: API Key Activity Logging for News Providers
description: Creates a public.news_api_keys table to store provider API keys, usage counters, failure counts, and status. Enables row-level security with an admin policy and adds SECURITY DEFINER RPC functions to atomically increment call and failure counters. Includes an event-trigger function that automatically attaches an AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE row-level trigger to newly created tables for logging activity.

title: Admin Truncate and Sequence Reset with Activity Logging
description: Adds admin functions to (1) truncate any specified table with CASCADE and record the operation in activity_logs, and (2) reset the table’s id sequence back to 1 (when found), also logging the action for auditing purposes.

title: Acivity log trigger function
description: Upgrades the audit trigger function to be more resilient. It adds logic to safely extract primary identifiers from various ID formats (id, user_id, or key) to prevent errors when logging tables with different structures.

title: Activity log and automatic trigger
description: Sets up dynamic audit triggers that automatically log all INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations across all public tables into the activity log, ensuring a complete history of data mutations.

title: List Public Base Table Names
sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_all_tables()
RETURNS TABLE(table_name text)
LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY SELECT t.table_name::text
    FROM information_schema.tables t
      WHERE t.table_schema = 'public' AND t.table_type = 'BASE TABLE';
      END; $$;
description: Provides an RPC function ('get_all_tables') that dynamically queries the information schema to list all base tables in the public schema, used for administrative tools and database management.

title: User Gallery Items with RLS Access Control
sql:
CREATE TABLE public.gallery_items (
      id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
          user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
              url TEXT NOT NULL,
                  type TEXT NOT NULL,
                      mime_type TEXT,
                          filename TEXT,
                              size BIGINT,
                                  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
                                      width INTEGER,
                                          height INTEGER,
                                              duration INTEGER
                                              );

                                              -- Security Policies (RLS)
                                              ALTER TABLE public.gallery_items ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

                                              CREATE POLICY "Users can view their own gallery items"
                                                  ON public.gallery_items FOR SELECT
                                                      USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

                                                      CREATE POLICY "Users can insert their own gallery items"
                                                          ON public.gallery_items FOR INSERT
                                                              WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);

                                                              CREATE POLICY "Users can update their own gallery items"
                                                                  ON public.gallery_items FOR UPDATE
                                                                      USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

                                                                      CREATE POLICY "Users can delete their own gallery items"
                                                                          ON public.gallery_items FOR DELETE
                                                                              USING (auth.uid() = user_id);
description: Implements the 'gallery_items' table to manage user-uploaded media. It tracks comprehensive metadata including dimensions, duration for videos, file sizes, and MIME types for a rich gallery experience.

title: Dairy Items, Entries & Payments Schema
description: Optimizes the dairy tracking system with a robust ledger structure. It includes improved relationship handling, standardized UUID generation, and explicit RLS policies for every operation type.

title: Dairy Inventory, Entries, and Payments Schema
sql:
-- Create Dairy Items Table
create table if not exists public.dairy_items (
  id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id uuid references auth.users(id) on delete cascade not null,
      name text not null,
        default_price numeric not null default 0,
          unit text not null default 'liter',
            created_at timestamptz default now()
            );

            -- Create Dairy Entries Table
            create table if not exists public.dairy_entries (
              id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
                user_id uuid references auth.users(id) on delete cascade not null,
                  item_id uuid references public.dairy_items(id) on delete cascade not null,
                    quantity numeric not null default 1,
                      price_per_unit numeric not null,
                        total_price numeric not null,
                          entry_date date not null default current_date,
                            is_paid boolean default false,
                              note text,
                                created_at timestamptz default now()
                                );

                                -- Create Dairy Payments Table
                                create table if not exists public.dairy_payments (
                                  id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
                                    user_id uuid references auth.users(id) on delete cascade not null,
                                      amount numeric not null,
                                        payment_date date not null default current_date,
                                          note text,
                                            created_at timestamptz default now()
                                            );

                                            -- Enable RLS
                                            alter table public.dairy_items enable row level security;
                                            alter table public.dairy_entries enable row level security;
                                            alter table public.dairy_payments enable row level security;

                                            -- Create Policies
                                            create policy "Users can manage their own dairy items" on public.dairy_items
                                              for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

                                              create policy "Users can manage their own dairy entries" on public.dairy_entries
                                                for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

                                                create policy "Users can manage their own dairy payments" on public.dairy_payments
                                                  for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);
description: Creates specialized tables ('dairy_items', 'dairy_entries', 'dairy_payments') for tracking commodity purchases. It handles unit-based pricing, payment status, and historical notes for daily supplies.

title: Voice Conversations Storage Setup
description: Sets up the 'voice_conversations' storage bucket and matches it with a 'conversations' table. This infrastructure allows the app to store and retrieve voice-to-voice interaction recordings securely.

title: User Vehicles & Transaction Metadata Setup
description: Creates a 'vehicles' table for tracking fleet data (cars/bikes) and adds a flexible 'metadata' JSONB column to transactions to support specialized entries like fuel logs and odometer readings.

title: Add Location JSONB to Finance Transactions
sql:
ALTER TABLE public.finance_transactions 
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS location JSONB;
description: Extends financial transactions with a 'location' JSONB column, enabling the storage of structured geographic data or merchant address info for every expense or income entry.

title: Finance Profiles and Transactions Schema
description: Introduces the 'finance_profiles' table, allowing users to separate their transactions into different accounts (e.g., Personal, Business). It also migrates the transactions table to link every entry to a specific profile.

title: User Finance Transactions Table with RLS
sql:
-- 1. Create the finance_transactions table
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.finance_transactions (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
        transaction_date TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
          amount NUMERIC(12, 2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
            type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('expense', 'income', 'transfer')),
              category TEXT,
                description TEXT,
                  payment_method TEXT DEFAULT 'Cash'
                  );

                  -- 2. Add a comment for documentation
                  COMMENT ON TABLE public.finance_transactions IS 'Stores financial transactions for the user.';

                  -- 3. Enable Row Level Security (RLS)
                  ALTER TABLE public.finance_transactions ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

                  -- 4. Create a policy so users can only access their own data
                  -- This single policy covers SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE
                  CREATE POLICY "Users can manage their own transactions" 
                  ON public.finance_transactions
                  FOR ALL 
                  USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

                  -- 5. (Optional) Create an index on user_id and date for faster queries
                  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_finance_user_date 
                  ON public.finance_transactions(user_id, transaction_date);
description: Establishes the foundation for financial tracking with the 'finance_transactions' table. It supports categorization, payment methods, and amount tracking with precise numeric types and RLS protection.

title: User Notes Storage with RLS
sql:
-- ========= NOTES TABLE =========
-- Stores user personal notes with rich text, tags, and theming.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.notes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
        updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
          title TEXT,
            content TEXT,
              tags TEXT[], -- Array of strings for categorization
                is_pinned BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
                  color_theme TEXT DEFAULT 'default' -- For UI styling (e.g., 'amber', 'blue', 'green')
                  );

                  -- Add comment for documentation
                  COMMENT ON TABLE public.notes IS 'Stores user personal notes.';

                  -- Enable Row Level Security (RLS)
                  ALTER TABLE public.notes ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

                  -- Policy: Allow users to manage (CRUD) ONLY their own notes
                  CREATE POLICY "Users can manage their own notes." ON public.notes
                    FOR ALL USING (auth.uid() = user_id);
description: Sets up a comprehensive 'notes' table for personal user data. It supports rich text content, tagging arrays, pinning functionality, and UI color themes, all protected by strict user-level RLS.

title: User UI Customization Settings
description: Adds UI customization preferences to the 'user_settings' table, allowing users to persist their choice of application theme (light/dark/system), font family, and layout density.

title: Function Execution Statistics
sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_function_stats()
RETURNS TABLE(
  function_name text,
    total_calls bigint,
      success_count bigint,
        error_count bigint,
          last_run timestamptz
          ) AS $$
          BEGIN
            RETURN QUERY
              SELECT
                  'News Updater'::text as function_name,
                      COUNT(*) as total_calls,
                          COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'SUCCESS') as success_count,
                              COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status != 'SUCCESS') as error_count,
                                  MAX(created_at) as last_run
                                    FROM update_news_logs;
                                    END;
                                    $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

                                    GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION get_function_stats() TO authenticated;
                                    GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION get_function_stats() TO service_role;
description: Implements 'get_function_stats', an RPC function that aggregates performance history from news update logs to provide a summary of total calls, success rates, and the timestamp of the last successful run.

title: Real-Time Table Statistics Function
sql:
-- Function to get real-time database statistics
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_database_analytics()
RETURNS TABLE(
  table_name text,
    live_rows bigint,
      total_inserts bigint,
        total_updates bigint,
          total_deletes bigint
          ) AS $$
          BEGIN
            RETURN QUERY
              SELECT
                  relname::text as table_name,
                      n_live_tup as live_rows,
                          n_tup_ins as total_inserts,
                              n_tup_upd as total_updates,
                                  n_tup_del as total_deletes
                                    FROM pg_stat_user_tables
                                      WHERE schemaname = 'public'
                                        ORDER BY n_tup_ins DESC;
                                        END;
                                        $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

                                        -- Permissions setup
                                        REVOKE EXECUTE ON FUNCTION get_database_analytics() FROM PUBLIC;
                                        GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION get_database_analytics() TO service_role;
                                        GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION get_database_analytics() TO authenticated;
description: Defines a diagnostic function ('get_database_analytics') that returns real-time statistics for all public tables, including live row counts and total mutation operations (inserts, updates, deletes).

title: Atomic Article Stat Increment
description: Provides a PL/pgSQL function ('increment_article_stat') that performs atomic increments on engagement counters (views, likes, bookmarks) within JSONB structures, ensuring data integrity during high-concurrency updates.

title: Add Engagement Metrics to News Articles
description: Creates the 'user_article_interactions' table to track which articles an individual user has liked or bookmarked. This supports personalized feeds and ensures engagement states are persisted per user.

title: Add Engagement Metrics to News Articles
sql:
-- Adds columns for views, likes, and bookmarks to the public news articles table.
ALTER TABLE public.public_news_articles
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS views INT DEFAULT 0,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS likes INT DEFAULT 0,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks INT DEFAULT 0;
description: Enhances the news articles schema by adding atomic counters for views, likes, and bookmarks, allowing the system to track article popularity and engagement metrics.

title: Update News Function Run Logs
description: Creates the 'update_news_logs' table to monitor the execution of the news updater Edge Function. It tracks success/failure status, duration, and provides a structured summary of each run for administrative auditing.

title: Article Conversation History
description: Implements a 'public_article_cache' table to store extracted content from any URL. This acts as a global cache that prevents redundant fetching and processing of the same articles by different users, improving system efficiency.

title: Public News Articles Table
description: Refines the 'public_news_articles' table structure to include a 'formatted_content_md' column. This allows the storage of Gemini-optimized markdown summaries alongside raw article data for a better reading experience.

title: Article Conversation History
description: Sets up the 'article_conversations' table to store AI-powered chat histories related to specific news articles. It includes a unique constraint to ensure each user maintains a single conversation thread per article URL.

title: Central Public News Articles Table
description: Creates the 'public_news_articles' table to store news content centrally for all users. It enables public read access via RLS and restricts write permissions to the 'service_role', allowing automated edge functions to update news data securely.

title: User Settings Storage with RLS Automation
sql:
-- ========= USER SETTINGS TABLE =========
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.user_settings (
  user_id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  api_key TEXT,
  voice_mode_voice TEXT,
  voice_mode_persona_instruction TEXT,
  voice_mode_tone_instruction TEXT,
  voice_mode_custom_instruction TEXT,
  voice_proactive_mode BOOLEAN,
  translator_usage JSONB
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.user_settings IS 'Stores individual settings for each user.';

-- Enable Row Level Security (safe)
ALTER TABLE public.user_settings ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- ========= POLICIES =========
DO $$ 
BEGIN
  IF NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM pg_policies 
    WHERE tablename = 'user_settings' 
    AND policyname = 'Users can manage their own settings.'
  ) THEN
    CREATE POLICY "Users can manage their own settings."
    ON public.user_settings
    FOR ALL
    USING (auth.uid() = user_id);
  END IF;
END $$;

-- ========= FUNCTION =========
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  -- Insert into profiles (safe)
  INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, full_name, avatar_url)
  VALUES (
    new.id,
    new.raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name',
    new.raw_user_meta_data->>'avatar_url'
  )
  ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

  -- Insert into user_settings (safe)
  INSERT INTO public.user_settings (user_id)
  VALUES (new.id)
  ON CONFLICT (user_id) DO NOTHING;

  RETURN new;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

-- ========= TRIGGER =========
DO $$
BEGIN
  IF NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM pg_trigger 
    WHERE tgname = 'on_auth_user_created'
  ) THEN
    CREATE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created
    AFTER INSERT ON auth.users
    FOR EACH ROW
    EXECUTE PROCEDURE public.handle_new_user();
  END IF;
END $$;
description: Recreates the 'user_settings' table with structured columns for API keys, voice assistant preferences, and translator usage. It also implements a robust 'handle_new_user' trigger that ensures both profile and settings entries are created automatically when a new user signs up.

title: Public user uploads bucket with RLS policies
sql:
-- Create a new bucket for user file uploads and make it public.
INSERT INTO storage.buckets (id, name, public)
VALUES ('user_uploads', 'user_uploads', true)
ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

-- RLS for 'user_uploads' bucket
-- Policy: Allow public read access to all uploaded files.
CREATE POLICY "Uploaded files are publicly accessible."
ON storage.objects FOR SELECT
USING ( bucket_id = 'user_uploads' );

-- Policy: Allow authenticated users to upload files into their own folder.
-- The path in the app should be: `${user.id}/${...}`.
CREATE POLICY "Users can upload their own files."
ON storage.objects FOR INSERT
TO authenticated
WITH CHECK ( bucket_id = 'user_uploads' AND auth.uid() = (storage.foldername(name))[1]::uuid );

-- Policy: Allow users to delete their own files.
CREATE POLICY "Users can delete their own files."
ON storage.objects FOR DELETE
TO authenticated
USING ( bucket_id = 'user_uploads' AND auth.uid() = (storage.foldername(name))[1]::uuid );
description: Initializes a public storage bucket named 'user_uploads' for general file storage with RLS policies restricting users to their own folders. It also creates the initial 'user_settings' table to store per-user configuration as JSONB data.

title: Public user uploads bucket with RLS policies
sql:
-- Create profiles table first if it doesn't exist to prevent errors
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.profiles (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    full_name TEXT,
    avatar_url TEXT,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now())
);

-- Add new columns for full name and avatar URL to the profiles table safely
ALTER TABLE public.profiles ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS full_name TEXT;
ALTER TABLE public.profiles ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS avatar_url TEXT;

-- Update the function to handle the new 'full_name' field during user signup
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  -- Insert a new row into public.profiles, now including full_name
    INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, full_name, avatar_url)
      VALUES (new.id, new.raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name', new.raw_user_meta_data->>'avatar_url')
    ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
        RETURN new;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

-- == SUPABASE STORAGE SETUP FOR AVATARS ==

-- 1. Create a new bucket named 'avatars' and make it public
INSERT INTO storage.buckets (id, name, public)
VALUES ('avatars', 'avatars', true)
ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

-- 2. Set up Row Level Security (RLS) policies for the 'avatars' bucket

-- Policy: Allow anyone to view all avatars
CREATE POLICY "Avatar images are publicly accessible."
ON storage.objects FOR SELECT
USING ( bucket_id = 'avatars' );

-- Policy: Allow authenticated users to upload their own avatar
CREATE POLICY "Users can upload their own avatar."
ON storage.objects FOR INSERT
TO authenticated
WITH CHECK ( bucket_id = 'avatars' AND auth.uid() = (storage.foldername(name))[1]::uuid );

-- Policy: Allow users to update their own avatar
CREATE POLICY "Users can update their own avatar."
ON storage.objects FOR UPDATE
TO authenticated
USING ( bucket_id = 'avatars' AND auth.uid() = (storage.foldername(name))[1]::uuid );

-- Policy: Allow users to delete their own avatar
CREATE POLICY "Users can delete their own avatar."
ON storage.objects FOR DELETE
TO authenticated
USING ( bucket_id = 'avatars' AND auth.uid() = (storage.foldername(name))[1]::uuid );
description: Adds 'full_name' and 'avatar_url' columns to the profiles table. It also updates the 'handle_new_user' function to automatically populate these fields during signup and creates a public 'avatars' storage bucket with specific Row Level Security (RLS) policies allowing users to manage their own profile pictures.
